content:
# Underwater Enhancement Model Evaluation (2026-04-01)

Compared 3 models against WaterNet baseline on 3 test frames (3840x2160 DJI Action 4, deep/turbid water, resized to 1024px). CPU-only inference. Goal: warm subjects on blue water background (XHS dive aesthetic).

## Results Summary

| Model | Params | CPU Time/frame | R/G Ratio | Verdict |
|-------|--------|---------------|-----------|---------|
| WaterNet (baseline) | ~1M | 3.7s | 0.86 | Solid baseline. Subtle, natural. Slight noise amp (+20%). |
| FUnIE-GAN | 7.0M | 0.46s | 0.91 | **Best challenger.** 8x faster, better red recovery, best blue preservation. No noise amp. |
| Shallow-UWnet | 219K | 1.5s | 0.98 | **Rejected.** Severe desaturation (-61%) — gray/washed output. |
| Deep SESR | 2.46M | 12.5s | 0.97 | **Rejected.** Purple/lavender water tint. 3.4x slower. TF dependency. |

## Key Findings
- FUnIE-GAN's fully-convolutional UNet runs at native 1024px (critical — 256px resize destroys detail)
- Shallow-UWnet trained for in-air restoration, not underwater grading — strips underwater character
- Deep SESR denoises well (-15%) but purple tint in water background is a dealbreaker
- FUnIE-GAN edges ahead of WaterNet on speed (8x), red recovery (0.91 vs 0.86), and blue water preservation (B/G 1.19 vs 1.10)

## Test artifacts
- Results: working/sea_thru_poc/results/{01,02,03}_{waternet,funie_gan,shallow_uwnet,deep_sesr}.png
- Scripts: working/sea_thru_poc/run_funie_gan_hires.py, run_shallow_uwnet.py, run_deep_sesr.py
- Model repos: working/sea_thru_poc/models/{FUnIE-GAN,Shallow-UWnet,Deep-SESR}/
